title: System Response to External Input: The Zero-State Response
summary: This page shows that an accumulator and a backward difference system are inverses of each other by deriving their impulse responses and verifying that their convolution equals δ[n]. It then introduces the result that if an LTID system maps x[n] to y[n], it also maps the accumulated input \sum_{k=-\infty}^{n} x[k] to the accumulated output \sum_{k=-\infty}^{n} y[k].
